description: OFD1 centriole and centriolar satellite protein [Source:HGNC Symbol;Acc:HGNC:2567]
ciliary_role: Associated with oral-facial- digital type I syndrome. Absence of the Ofd1 protein leads to basal body dysfunction, which in turn prevents the assembly of the primary cilium, and causes failure of left-right axis specification (16311594). It associates with the distal ends of centriolar  to build distal appendages, recruit Ift88, and stabilise centriole length and regulate ciliogenesis (20230748). Its presence at the centriolar satellite suppresses primary ciliogenesis and removal of OFD1 by autophagy promotes it (24089205).
ciliopathy_type: Joubert Syndrome, Orofaciodigital Syndrome, Primary Ciliary Dyskinesia, Retinitis Pigmentosa, Simpson-Golabi-Behmel Syndrome
